description: Gateway attB1 minimal attachment site; 25 bp, the specificity-1 attB recombination site appended to a DNA fragment through the attB1 PCR primer tail. It recombines with attP1 (BP reaction) to move the fragment into a pDONR entry vector. An engineered lambda-derived site; the canonical sequence is from Hartley, Temple & Brasch 2000.

references.authors: Hartley JL, Temple GF, Brasch MA
references.title: DNA cloning using in vitro site-specific recombination

label: Gateway attB1
note: Gateway recombinational-cloning attB site, specificity 1; 25 bp minimal attachment site appended to a DNA fragment via the attB1 PCR primer tail. The BP reaction (lambda Int + IHF) recombines attB1 with attP1 to insert the fragment into a pDONR entry vector. Engineered lambda-derived site; canonical sequence per Hartley, Temple & Brasch 2000.

provenance.sequence_source: Gateway attB1 minimal recombination site as defined in Hartley, Temple & Brasch 2000 (PMID 11076863) and the Gateway cloning manual; engineered site (no natural deposit) - sequence from the defining publication

functional_claims.label: Gateway attB1 is the minimal (~25 bp) specificity-1 attachment site appended to a PCR product via the attB1 primer tail; it recombines with attP1 (BP reaction, lambda Int + IHF) to insert the product into a pDONR entry vector.
attB1 carries the specificity-1 central overlap and recombines only with the cognate attP1, giving the orthogonality that makes Gateway cloning directional.
Added as a primer tail when amplifying a gene for Gateway entry: 5'-GGGGACAAGTTTGTACAAAAAAGCAGGCT-[gene]; the 5' GGGG clamp protects the site during the recombination reaction.
